title: aquilegia atropurpurea var. dahurica
intro: Aquilegia atropurpurea var. dahurica is a lesser-known variety of the Columbine plant, belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are used to treat various health issues. The roots of Aquilegia atropurpurea var. dahurica have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be used as a tea, tincture, or infusion to harness its health benefits, including soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system.
